OSINTdefender Countries are seemingly gearing up for the expected uptempo in U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iranian energy and power infrastructure, as the Indian Ministry of External Affairs has released an advisory to Indian nationals in Iran, telling them to avoid being in close proximity to electric installations, military installations, and multi-story buildings for the next 48 hours. tweet

Faytuks News The U.S. Embassy in Bahrain has directed all U.S. government employees to shelter in place. They recommend that all Americans in Bahrain do the same until further notice. Bahrain: We are monitoring the situation in the Middle East closely and will keep you updated so you can make decisions about your safety. The U.S. Embassy in Manama has directed all U.S. government employees to shelter in place. We recommend all Americans in Bahrain do the same until further notice. To the extent possible, remain in a secure structure, and stay away from windows. Have a supply of food, water, medications, and other essential items. Please continue to check your email for additional information or last-minute changes. The U.S. Embassy Manama has suspended all routine consular services. Americans should contact ManamaConsular@state.gov in case of emergency. Read more: https://bh.usembassy.gov/security-alert-u-s-embassy-manama-bahrain-april-6-2026/ - TravelGov tweet
OSINTtechnical Iran has cut off direct communications with the U.S. over Trump’s “civilization will die” threat this morning. -WSJ tweet

Open Source Intel Some Key Iranian rail and road infrastructures reportedly hit: 1. Tehran: Tehran-Mashhad rail line (Qalaeno, Ray) struck 2. Isfahan: Yahyaabad-Kashan railway bridge attacked 3. Qom: West province land route targeted 4. Alborz: Karaj railway hit 5. Zanjan: Zanjan-Miane railway bridge targeted 6. East Azerbaijan: Tabriz-Tehran highway temporarily blocked, now reopened 7. Train cancellations: Mashhad and southern/Zagros lines until further notice tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇺🇸🇵🇰 NYT: Iran has stopped negotiation efforts with the United States and informed Pakistan that it will no longer engage in ceasefire talks, according to three senior Iranian officials. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 WSJ : Iran has cut off direct communications with the United States following U.S. President Trump’s threat to destroy “a whole civilisation”, though talks with ceasefire mediators continue. The move has temporarily complicated efforts to reach a deal by Trump’s Tuesday 8pm deadline but has not ended the talks. Iran intended to send a signal of defiance by severing direct contact. @wfwitness
